metadata.title: Free The Em Dash
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929; SPARK #D97757 one accent.
metadata.author: Nina Harris
metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nina Harris — Co-Founder, Humanitarians AI. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. A colleague asked to remove em dashes from an email because they supposedly look like AI wrote them. The punctuation itself never changed; only the cultural reading of it shifted. Emily Dickinson relied on em dashes so heavily her poetry barely functions without them. Punishing a centuries-old mark over AI anxiety means abandoning a genuine tool of human voice.

props.artifactTitle: Em dashes aren't an AI tell
artifactLines: A colleague asked to remove em dashes from an email because they supposedly look like AI wrote them.
The punctuation itself never changed; only the cultural reading of it shifted.
Emily Dickinson relied on em dashes so heavily her poetry barely functions without them.
Punishing a centuries-old mark over AI anxiety means abandoning a genuine tool of human voice.

beats.narration_text: I keep hearing that em dashes now signal AI authorship, but Dickinson built whole poems around them long before any model existed. I want to test whether this is real bias or just vibes. Help me: (1) collect ten of my own old emails from before 2022 and count my natural em-dash rate; (2) rewrite three current drafts once with em dashes and once without, then A/B which reads warmer; (3) draft a short defense I can send my colleague explaining why the punctuation predates the panic.

output: We use Claude, ChatGPT, Midjourney, n8n, V0

beats.narration_text: So when I recently drafted an email to a colleague and was told to take out the em dashes because they “look like AI wrote it,” I had a moment. The kind where you realize a cultural shift has happened while you weren’t paying attention, and now you’re on the wrong side of it.

beats.narration_text: The punctuation mark didn’t change. And now we’re punishing ourselves for it.

beats.narration_text: Dickinson didn’t just use em dashes. It barely works with anything else.
